Accordion.description: An accordion is a UI component that displays a vertical stack of collapsible panels.

Action bar.description: The Action Bar is a UI component designed to display a set of actions in a single row, providing users with quick access to key functions.

Action sheet.description: An Action Sheet is a bottom-aligned overlay that presents a set of contextual actions.

App bar.description: The App Bar (or Navigation Header) is a top-anchored component that helps establish the context of the current screen and allows users to navigate through different flows.

Banners.description: The PacificBanner component is a versatile UI element used to display important messages.

Bottom nav bar.description: The Bottom Navigation component provides users with quick access to key sections of the app, improving navigation efficiency and accessibility.

Bottom sheet.description: A Bottom Sheet is a surface that slides up from the bottom of the screen to present additional content, actions, or contextual information.

Button groups v1.description: Vertical and horizontal stacking of buttons defining padding, spacing, and position of primary, secondary, and tertiary buttons

Button groups v2.description: A Button Group is a UI component that clusters a set of related buttons together, either in a horizontal or vertical stack, to create a single, cohesive unit.

Buttons v1.description: Interactive elements that allow users to perform actions or trigger functions

Buttons v2.description: A button component is a fundamental interactive element in a user interface that allows users to perform an action or trigger an event.

Card container.description: Cards are used to contain related information.

Checkbox.description: Checkboxes are designed for multiple choices, not for mutually exclusive options.

Control pill.description: The Control Pill is an interactive, pill-shaped element used to display and edit contextual settings—like account selection, coin switching, or stock selection—without navigating away from the current screen.

Date picker.description: The Date Picker component enables users to select a date or date range through an intuitive calendar interface, making date selection quick and easy.

Dialog.description: Dialogs are modal windows that interrupt the current flow to present important information or require user action.

Dropdown inputs.description: Dropdown components are a special type of text input.

File uploader.description: The File uploader component allows users to upload file(s) from their device using a native file input or by dragging and dropping files into a designated drop zone.

Filter pill.description: Filter pill or item uses tags or descriptive words to narrow down or organize content based on specific criteria.

Floating Action Button FAB.description: A Floating Action Button (FAB) can serve as a navigational element that represents a primary, high-priority action that often leads users to key destinations.

Global Header.description: The Global Header appears at the top of the page and remains consistent across the product.

Keypad.description: The keypad component allows users to input numerical values in a structured and intuitive manner.

Legalese.description: The Legalese Component is designed to display legal disclaimers, terms, or agreements in a structured and user-friendly manner.

Loaders.description: Loaders are UI elements that notify users that an application is performing an action in the background.

Marquee input.description: Input components are interactive elements that collect data from users.

Marquee.description: The static Marquee is a large, visually prominent typographic element used to introduce key content on a page or within a section.

Page header.description: The header-page component is a typographic header component that defines the spacing and dimension relationship between a page title and subtitle.

Pagination.description: Pagination is a component that breaks large sets of data or content into smaller, easier-to-navigate pages.

Performance pill.description: The Performance Pill is a compact visual indicator that communicates changes in numerical data, such as stock holdings fluctuating in value.

Pill group.description: A Pill Group arranges a small set of pills into a single, scannable control cluster with shared behavior and layout rules.

Progress meters.description: This page focuses on guidelines and documentation for Progress Meter components.

Radio.description: Radio buttons are used for mutually exclusive choices, not for multiple choices.

Rows Small.description: The Small Row is an alternative to the Standard Row where a condensed and contained layout is required.

Rows.description: The PacificRow is a key structural element used to display a collection of related data points in a clean, easy-to-read format.

Scroll picker.description: The scrollPicker component is used to display one or more scrollable lists of values from which users can choose.

Search input.description: The Search Input component is a special type of text-based input that allows users to quickly locate specific information within large datasets, such as transactions, holdings, accounts, or stocks.

Section header.description: The Section Header component is a typographic component that introduces and labels distinct sections of content, helping to establish a clear visual hierarchy.

Segmented control.description: A segmentedControl is a linear set of 2-3 options with one segment selected at a time.

Selector pill.description: The Selector Pill is a compact, pill-shaped element used to represent a selectable context, category, or mode.

Selector tile.description: Selector Tiles offer users clear, scannable options to choose from, presenting relevant and actionable information in a compact and visually appealing format.

Slide to Action button.description: The Slide to {Action} or Slide Button is a high-intent interactive component that requires users to slide a draggable handle from one side to the other to complete an action.

Sliders.description: Sliders let users select a value or range of values along a defined scale.

Status pill.description: The Status Pill is a simple visual element that helps users quickly understand important information.

Steppers.description: This documentation focuses on vertical and horizontal steppers.

Sub header.description: A subHeader is used to visually group and separate related content by date.

Table.description: Tables organize and present data in a structured, easy-to-scan layout.

Tabs.description: Tabs allows users to toggle between different views or subsets within an application or webpage.

Task list.description: A Task List component displays a list of tasks or items, often with checkboxes or status indicators, allowing users to track progress, mark tasks as complete, or take action on individual items.

Text inputs.description: Text Inputs are UI elements that allow users to input and edit text.

Toast.description: A Toast is a brief, non-intrusive notification that confirms the outcome of a recent action or process.

Toggle.description: Toggles (aka Switch) let users turn an individual option on or off, typically for activating or deactivating specific settings.

Tooltip.description: Our Tooltip component is a small, contextual pop-up that provides brief, informative text when a user hovers over, focuses on, or interacts with an element.
